mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output; this output growth translates into higher corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in > 55% of months over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate persistently above 6% signals excess labor market slack that depresses wage growth and consumer spending, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered months over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month payroll employment growth and a federal funds rate below 0.25% generate a non-linear reflationary impulse: rising PAYEMS signals earnings expansion while the low FEDFUNDS compresses the equity discount rate, jointly producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of rising PAYEMS and FEDFUNDS below 0.25% fail to produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in > 60% of instances over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In high-unemployment regimes, month-over-month increases in consumer prices signal cost-push inflation that squeezes corporate profit margins without offsetting demand strength, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.0% and CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales reflects robust consumer expenditure that translates directly into corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month RSAFS growth coincides with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in > 55% of months over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production combined with a federal funds rate at the effective lower bound signals a liquidity trap where monetary policy cannot offset negative supply shocks, triggering rapid risk-off repricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of falling INDPRO and FEDFUNDS below 0.25% predict positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in > 50% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,500 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and forward-looking construction activity, which drags aggregate demand and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1,500, positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in > 50% of triggered instances during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate over a 3-month window signals a tightening labor market that accelerates wage-driven consumption and corporate pricing power, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: A 3-month decline in UNRATE coincides with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in > 55% of episodes over a rolling 3-year window.
